summary: Combat action cards need explicit routing fields rather than sheet-side guessing.
riskIfSkipped: Manual/reference actions will keep becoming fake executable buttons and real attack actions will lose context.

summary: The action economy engine should enforce RAW substitution direction and full-round action cost before combat states spend actions.
riskIfSkipped: States and actions may spend impossible combinations, including full-round actions leaving swift actions available.

summary: Build a canonical attack context so Aim, Charge, Disarm, Autofire, Burst Fire, firing into melee, and weapon modes reach the resolver consistently.
riskIfSkipped: Feat/talent metadata will remain unable to wake up because context names and storage are inconsistent.

summary: Attack chat cards and damage buttons must preserve enough context for crits, area attacks, Burst Fire, Evasion, Stun, Ion, Sonic, and damage riders.
riskIfSkipped: Rolls may be correct but damage will be wrong or incomplete.

summary: Damage should be represented as typed packets instead of flat labels to support Stun, Ion, Sonic, Fire, Acid, Evasion, and mixed damage.
riskIfSkipped: Special damage rules will become one-off hacks and regressions will be likely.

summary: Full Attack, Double Attack, Triple Attack, and dual weapon fighting should route through the existing full attack executor.
riskIfSkipped: Dual weapon and multiattack support will remain present in code but unreliable from the player-facing sheet.

summary: Autofire should automate attack penalty and ammo but leave area placement and affected targets to the GM.
riskIfSkipped: Autofire may either over-automate map details or under-automate rules-critical hit/miss damage behavior.

summary: Burst Fire must be modeled as a standard-action single-target attack, not an area attack and never half damage on miss.
riskIfSkipped: Burst Fire can incorrectly inherit Autofire area behavior or Evasion interactions.

summary: When ammo tracking is enabled, show shot pills, reload buttons, and ammo gates. When disabled, remove all ammo UI clutter.
riskIfSkipped: Players can select Burst/Autofire with insufficient shots or see irrelevant ammo controls when the houserule disables ammo counting.

summary: Stun and Ion need half-HP damage packets with original damage retained for Damage Threshold checks and target-type gated effects.
riskIfSkipped: Stun and Ion will behave like normal damage and condition effects will be wrong.

summary: Sonic counts as Energy but ranged Sonic cannot be deflected, and mixed Sonic bonus damage needs packet-level handling.
riskIfSkipped: Lightsaber Deflect may incorrectly block or fail to block Sonic damage packets.

summary: Fight Defensively and Total Defense need RAW action cost, attack restriction, and Acrobatics-trained +5/+10 Reflex handling.
riskIfSkipped: Defense bonuses will be wrong and may be free toggles rather than action-costed choices.

summary: Grabbed, Grappled, and Pinned need a RAW-safe state machine that preserves Pin/Trip gating, meet-beats, escape rules, and correct defense effects.
riskIfSkipped: Grapple will remain one of the highest combat fidelity risks.

summary: Aid Another should roll the attempt and provide an apply +2 button or pending bonus rather than only a chat result.
riskIfSkipped: Aid Another will remain informative but not useful mechanically.

summary: Healing, Bonus HP, Treat Injury, natural healing, droid repair, vehicle repair, and biotech repair need separate operation packets.
riskIfSkipped: Organic healing, droid repair, gear use, and Bonus HP will remain inconsistent.

summary: NPC and follower sheets should consume the same combat routing and packets instead of getting a parallel implementation.
riskIfSkipped: The fixed player combat path will diverge from NPC/follower combat again.
